name: Galileo
first: First spacecraft to orbit Jupiter; atmospheric probe into Jupiter clouds
description: Launched from Space Shuttle Atlantis after the long post-Challenger pause, Galileo braved the inner asteroid belt and conducted the first long-term study of Jupiter and its moons from orbit. It discovered Europa's induced magnetic field — strong evidence of a subsurface ocean — and watched Shoemaker-Levy 9 slam into Jupiter. The mission ended with a controlled plunge into Jupiter in 2003 to avoid contaminating icy moons that might harbour conditions for life.

events.label: LAUNCH
events.note: STS-34 deploys Galileo and an inertial upper stage from Atlantis.

events.label: JUPITER ORBIT INSERTION
events.note: Main engine burn captures Galileo into orbit; decades of moon science begin.
